capability_objective: The clinic scheduler is checking two appointment records for the front-desk handoff. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the appointment named “Annual Vision Screening” at “Maple Clinic” and the appointment named “Vaccination Review” at “Orchard Clinic”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use exactly three bullets: one per record in the requested order, then one status-comparison bullet; include both stable IDs.
prompt:
The clinic scheduler needs a read-only appointment audit for the front-desk handoff. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `appointment-audit.md` from full appointment records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `APPOINTMENTS.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./appointmentctl` executable for every appointment-registry lookup; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the appointment named “Annual Vision Screening” at “Maple Clinic” and the appointment named “Vaccination Review” at “Orchard Clinic”. Your first appointment-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./appointmentctl` processes, with no other appointment operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following appointment-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./appointmentctl` processes. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take every reported stable ID and status for a resolved branch only from its successful full-record get.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter appointment data. Do not expose dates, clinicians, notes, unrelated matches, or other full-record fields in the deliverable.

`appointment-audit.md` must contain exactly three nonblank Markdown bullet lines and end every line with a newline. The first two bullets must each be exactly one sentence, in the requested appointment order, using this wording: `- <name> at <location> has stable ID <id> and retrieved status <status>.` For an unresolved branch, instead write exactly `- <name> at <location> is unresolved.` The third and final bullet must be exactly one status-comparison sentence: if both retrieved statuses are present and identical, write `- Status comparison: both are <status>.`; if both are present and differ, write `- Status comparison: <first status> differs from <second status>.`; otherwise write exactly `- Status comparison: unknown.` Substitute only values obtained through the permitted workflow, include no placeholders or angle brackets, and add no heading, blank line, commentary, or recommendation. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Return only the same three bullets in your final response.
